inline_comments.message: no need to put this s in brackets

given: 'an endpoint for returning CM-Handles for a registered dmi plugin'
a collection of CM-Handles are returned

NB: I was wondering , instead of returning an array of string , what if we create an object and return that instead.
So for the moment we can have just cmhandleId as an attribute but it will be easily extendable  without caring about the backward compatibility later on. Just thinking..

Team , what do you think??

In the Jira it says its supposed to be all the cm handle details. Did this scope change? If not it should probably be an array of RestOutputCmHandles. If its just the ids I think it should just be a list of strings and the object can always be extended after

I don't think this output is correct? should be:
["cm-handle-1", "cm-handle2"]
should this check for PNFDemo4 as well?

Yes for now we are only responding back with CM handle IDs until we get clear information from Peter/Kieran on what exact information is needed. 

I agree with Priyank returning an Object for extendability

i think it is correct response. next patch will be different after changing it to an object
